coverage.name: Bloom Energy
coverage.verdict_label: Held both books - catalyst long, now the drag
coverage.thesis: **Our one live catalyst long - and today the drag.** Live 3 sh @ $222.64, paper 40 sh @ $218.90; $188 GTC stop confirmed on each. It gapped UP to $219 at the open then PUKED to ~$200 (-6.6%) - a failed bounce giving back the $1.7B-financing pop as a Hunterbrook short report + supply-chain fears + profit-taking swamp it. The catalyst is real and specific (Oaktree/IDF $1.7B to deploy Bloom fuel cells for Nebius AI data-center power - energy-for-AI, decoupled from the chip fear) with Q2 earnings 7/28 next, but the chart is a knife: lower highs ($223->$219) testing the $194 (7/17 low) support with air below it. A catalyst swing on a wide stop - held, not sold at the low, but the #1 rotation candidate on any real bounce.
coverage.hold_reason: We own BE as the clearest catalyst name - a $1.7B Oaktree/Nebius deal to power AI data centers into 7/28 earnings - but today it's the drag: it popped to $219 at the open and dumped to ~$200 on the short-seller overhang, and it's the whole reason live is down. We're holding the wide $188 stop rather than sell at the low - the business thesis is intact and it's a high-beta name that can snap back 10% in a day. What we're watching: a bounce toward ~$210 is where we'd rotate it into a stronger name; a break of the $194 support lets the $188 stop do its job.
coverage.size_note: Own both books; $188 stop each. The drag today - hold the wide stop, rotate on a bounce, don't sell the low.

projection.basis: gapped to $219 and puked on the short overhang - battleground into 7/28
